MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Western Tug & Barge Corp.
Dept. 514. Judge: Eric R. Fleming. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Mitch L. Lyon, CSR #6840. Telephone: 415-551-3703.

James H. Ghilotti, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs Gary Smith and Linda Smith, who were not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defaulted defendant Western Tug & Barge Corp.
Plaintiffs' complaint was filed on March 9, 2017 and served on defendant Western Tug & Barge Corp. on August 8, 2017.
Statement of Damages was served on defendant Western Tug & Barge Corp. on August 8, 2017 and filed on September 14, 2017.
Default was entered against defendant Western Tug & Barge Corp. on September 14, 2017.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on August 9, 2018.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiffs Gary Smith and Linda Smith, and against the Defaulted Defendant Western Tug & Barge Corp., as follows:

Economic Damages for plaintiff Gary Smith - $560,647.48.
Non-Economic Damages for plaintiff Gary Smith - $10,000,000.00.
Loss of Consortium as to plaintiff Linda Smith - $10,000,000.00.
Total Economic & Non-Economic Damages as to defendant Western Tug & Barge Corp. -$20,560,647.48.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The November 5, 2018 trial date is advanced to June 11, 2018 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is June 21, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is June 7, 2018. "Traditional" Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on 35-day notice, (Traditional shall refer to exposure, causation, product identification and punitive damages, and 45-day notice period on all other summary judgment/adjudication motions, unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry.)

MINI MINUTES:
(PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes, continued from previous entry)
No later than March 12, 2018, plaintiffs must be produced for deposition for any defendant(s) served after the conclusion of their prior depositions. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than April 27, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than May 11, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by June 4, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on June 7, 2018. Fact discovery cut-off date is May 25, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is June 8, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than March 23, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
No later than March 7, 2018 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than March 7, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on April 18, 2018 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to CRC, Rule 3.1380 and San Francisco Local Rules of Court, Rules 5.0 and 20.2 on June 11, 2018 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rules 5.0 and 20.2 and provide a confidential settlement statement by June 7, 2018 at 2:00p.m. The oppositions filed by Puget Sound Commerce Center, Inc., Caterpillar Inc., and Genstar Pacific Corp. were not considered for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court.
